bip32_key_derivation.highlight: First BIP32 non-hardened derivation for lattice-based PQ (RaccoonG)
Gaussian distributions enable unlinkable child keys up to ~500k depth
Hash-based signatures cannot support extended public keys (non-hardened paths)

hardware_wallet_constraints.highlight: Current Ledger SE: 64KB RAM, 70MHz - tight for PQ schemes
Falcon-512 signing barely fits with OS overhead; Falcon-1024 doesn't run
ML-DSA runs with low-RAM implementation but 5+ second signing time
SLH-DSA signing: 6 minutes at 128-bit security - unacceptable UX

next_generation_hardware.highlight: Next-gen SE: 158KB RAM, Keccak IP, ML-KEM/ML-DSA hardware acceleration
No planned HW acceleration for Falcon or SLH-DSA

algorithm_viability.highlight: ML-DSA: confirmed next-gen, likely legacy devices (with side-channel protections TBD)
Falcon: feasible next-gen, uncertain legacy due to floating-point masking complexity
SLH-DSA: unlikely for signing on any current/next-gen hardware

implementation_approaches.highlight: Ephemeral mode compatible with lattice HD wallets for stateless reusable keys
Few-time signatures (Winternitz) viable for ephemeral use cases
Stateful hash schemes with stateless fallback under Bitcoin community consideration

action_items.action: Offline discussion on BIP85 + few-time signatures for ephemeral keys
Begin side-channel countermeasure work for ML-DSA and Falcon
